R1 Serial0/1/0 = 10.0.0.1/30 (desc: Link to R2) -- peer (router2) not reachable for confirmation
R3 Serial0/1/1 = 10.0.0.6/30 (desc: Link to R2) -- peer (router2) not reachable for confirmation

recent_changes.summary: Added/updated device R1 from running-config (interfaces and ios_xe_version).
Attempted fetch for router2 failed (no running-config); recorded connection error.
Added/updated device R3 from running-config (interfaces and ios_xe_version).
Inferred links: R1<->R3 (Serial0/1/1<->Serial0/1/0) and edges R1->router2 and R3->router2 (router2 unreachable).

facts.claim: R1 is running Cisco IOS XE 16.12
R3 is running Cisco IOS XE 16.12
router2 was unreachable during an attempted running-config fetch

root_causes.cause: Both R1 and R3 have static/host routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at each other (R1 -> 10.0.0.9, R3 -> 10.0.0.10). Traffic from 192.168.0.12 destined to 172.16.0.12 will be forwarded R1->R3 then R3->R1 instead of toward the actual network, producing a forwarding loop/blackhole and causing all pings to fail.
evidence: R1 routing entry: prefix 172.16.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0 fwd-list: fwd = 10.0.0.9 (ToolMessage: get_running_config for R1)
R3 routing entry: prefix 172.16.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0 fwd-list: fwd = 10.0.0.10 (ToolMessage: get_running_config for R3)
Interface addressing shows R1 Serial0/1/1 = 10.0.0.10 and R3 Serial0/1/0 = 10.0.0.9 (matching the above next-hops), so each router points the 172.16.0.0/24 next-hop at the other router.

root_causes.cause: The intermediate router (router2) that likely hosts or connects the 172.16.0.0/24 network is unreachable, so the true path to 172.16.0.12 is not available.
evidence: Topology and recent_changes show inferred links R1->router2 and R3->router2 but 'router2 was unreachable during an attempted running-config fetch' (facts and recent_changes).
get_running_config for router2 returned: { "error": "All connection attempts failed" } (ToolMessage)

R1 has Serial0/1/0 configured as 'Link to R2' (10.0.0.1/30) and R3 has Serial0/1/1 as 'Link to R2' (10.0.0.6/30) — the physical/adjacent router that would reach 172.16.0.0/24 cannot be reached via management/tooling.

data.intent_description: 192.168.0.12 cannot reach 172.16.0.12 (25/0 pings). Root cause: R1 and R3 have reciprocal static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other, causing a forwarding loop/blackhole; router2 (true path to 172.16.0.0/24) is currently unreachable. Desired outcome: restore correct routing so 192.168.0.12 can reach 172.16.0.12.

plan.problem: Mutual static routes on router1 and router3 point 172.16.0.0/24 at each other (next-hops 10.0.0.9 and 10.0.0.10), producing a forwarding loop. Router2 (the correct next-hop toward 172.16.0.0/24) was unreachable to tools; assumed router2 link addresses are 10.0.0.2 (toward router1) and 10.0.0.5 (toward router3). Missing info: definitive router2 next-hop addresses.
plan.fix_summary: On router1 and router3 remove the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 that points at the other router, and add static routes pointing toward the assumed router2 next-hops (router1->10.0.0.2, router3->10.0.0.5). Risk: if router2 remains down/unreachable, ICMP reachability may still fail.

Inferred links: R1<->R3 (Serial0/1/1<->Serial0/1/0) and edges R1->router2 and R3->router2 (router2 unreachable).

facts.claim: R1 is running Cisco IOS XE 16.12
R3 is running Cisco IOS XE 16.12
router2 was unreachable during an attempted running-config fetch

root_causes.cause: Both R1 and R3 have static/host routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at each other (R1 -> 10.0.0.9, R3 -> 10.0.0.10). Traffic from 192.168.0.12 destined to 172.16.0.12 will be forwarded R1->R3 then R3->R1 instead of toward the actual network, producing a forwarding loop/blackhole and causing all pings to fail.
evidence: R1 routing entry: prefix 172.16.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0 fwd-list: fwd = 10.0.0.9 (ToolMessage: get_running_config for R1)
R3 routing entry: prefix 172.16.0.0 mask 255.255.255.0 fwd-list: fwd = 10.0.0.10 (ToolMessage: get_running_config for R3)
Interface addressing shows R1 Serial0/1/1 = 10.0.0.10 and R3 Serial0/1/0 = 10.0.0.9 (matching the above next-hops), so each router points the 172.16.0.0/24 next-hop at the other router.

root_causes.cause: The intermediate router (router2) that likely hosts or connects the 172.16.0.0/24 network is unreachable, so the true path to 172.16.0.12 is not available.
evidence: Topology and recent_changes show inferred links R1->router2 and R3->router2 but 'router2 was unreachable during an attempted running-config fetch' (facts and recent_changes).
get_running_config for router2 returned: { "error": "All connection attempts failed" } (ToolMessage)

R1 has Serial0/1/0 configured as 'Link to R2' (10.0.0.1/30) and R3 has Serial0/1/1 as 'Link to R2' (10.0.0.6/30) — the physical/adjacent router that would reach 172.16.0.0/24 cannot be reached via management/tooling.

plan.problem: Mutual static routes on router1 and router3 point 172.16.0.0/24 at each other (next-hops 10.0.0.9 and 10.0.0.10), producing a forwarding loop. Router2 (the correct next-hop toward 172.16.0.0/24) was unreachable to tools; assumed router2 link addresses are 10.0.0.2 (toward router1) and 10.0.0.5 (toward router3). Missing info: definitive router2 next-hop addresses.
plan.fix_summary: On router1 and router3 remove the incorrect static route for 172.16.0.0/24 that points at the other router, and add static routes pointing toward the assumed router2 next-hops (router1->10.0.0.2, router3->10.0.0.5). Risk: if router2 remains down/unreachable, ICMP reachability may still fail.

data.intent_description: 192.168.0.12 cannot reach 172.16.0.12 (25/0 pings). Root cause: R1 and R3 have reciprocal static routes for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other, causing a forwarding loop/blackhole; router2 (true path to 172.16.0.0/24) is currently unreachable. Desired outcome: restore correct routing so 192.168.0.12 can reach 172.16.0.12.

messages.content:
What was observed
- Host 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 (all pings failed).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R1 and R3 each had a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at the other (R1 → 10.0.0.9, R3 → 10.0.0.10), creating a forwarding loop/blackhole. The true intermediate router (router2) was unreachable to management tooling.

Changes made
- On R1: removed static 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 and added 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.2.
- On R3: removed static 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.10 and added 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.5.

Verification
- Passed (configuration-level): R1 now shows 172.16.0.0/24 → 10.0.0.2 and R3 shows 172.16.0.0/24 → 10.0.0.5; R1 Serial0/1/1 operationally up.
- Remaining issues: router2 remains unreachable to the management tools (its operational state/running-config unknown). End-to-end host reachability (ping/traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12) was not run and is not confirmed; if router2 is down, traffic may still fail.

Recommended next step
- Confirm router2 is up and reachable (collect running-config/iface status) and perform an end-to-end ping/traceroute to validate connectivity.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- Host 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 (all pings failed).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R1 and R3 each had a static route for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at the other (R1 → 10.0.0.9, R3 → 10.0.0.10), creating a forwarding loop/blackhole. The true intermediate router (router2) was unreachable to management tooling.

- On R3: removed static 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.10 and added 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.5.

Verification
- Passed (configuration-level): R1 now shows 172.16.0.0/24 → 10.0.0.2 and R3 shows 172.16.0.0/24 → 10.0.0.5; R1 Serial0/1/1 operationally up.
- Remaining issues: router2 remains unreachable to the management tools (its operational state/running-config unknown). End-to-end host reachability (ping/traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12) was not run and is not confirmed; if router2 is down, traffic may still fail.

Recommended next step
- Confirm router2 is up and reachable (collect running-config/iface status) and perform an end-to-end ping/traceroute to validate connectivity.
